data.text: Patient Pradeep Pattnaik w/o Soumya Chatterjee, a 41-year-old male, presented at our hospital with complaint of shortness of breath. He was initially assessed by Dr. R.K. Mehta, Consultant Medicine. On examination, patient was found to be tachypneic with mild chest discomfort. His investigations revealed evidence of bronchitis. Patient was started on appropriate treatment.

Patient Anita Mishra s/o Arindam Ghosh, a 67-year-old female presents to the Neurology ward with severe headache. She complaints of sudden onset of severe headache of 36 hours duration associated with vomiting. She has a past medical history of hypertension and diabetes mellitus.

The patient, Neha Singh s/o Subramaniam Venkataraman, a 71-year-old female, presented to the outpatient department with a complaint of severe headache. She described it as a dull, aching pain localized to the frontal region, which has been worsening over the past two days. She has been experiencing occasional episodes of dizziness and has difficulty concentrating. She denies any history of trauma, fever, or other systemic complaints.

Signed, Dr. Arindam Ghosh Apollo Hospital Chennai 05 July 2022 Time: 14:00 hrs Apollo Hospital Chennai Prescription Note Date: 07/02/2024 Patient Details: - Name: Sunita Sharma s/o Debasis Roy - Age: 50-year-old - ABHA ID: 10-3946-4350-7220 Chief Complaint: Patient presents with complaint of chest pain. Medical History: Patient is known to have hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Vital Parameters: - Blood Pressure: 140/90 mmHg - Pulse Rate: 88/min - Respiratory Rate: 22/min - Temperature: 37°C Assessment: Patient's chest pain is likely due to angina pectoris. ECG is advised to rule out any cardiac ischemia. Patient is advised to avoid strenuous activities and follow a low-fat diet. Medications: 1. Aspirin 75 mg OD (oral, once daily) 2. Atorvastatin 20 mg OD 3. Losartan 50 mg BD (oral, twice daily) 4. Metformin 500 mg BD Dietary Advice: Patient is advised to follow a low-fat diet and avoid smoking. Investigations: - ECG to be done within 24 hours - Lipid profile to be done in 2 weeks - Echo to be done in 1 month Follow-up: Patient is advised to follow up after 2 weeks with the same doctor. Contact Details: Dr. R.K. Mehta Phone: 9702906583
- Name: Arindam Ghosh w/o Sunita Sharma - Age: 28-year-old - ABHA ID: 38-8019-9176-7039 - Phone: 9178727317

The patient, Sunita Sharma s/o Debasis Roy, presented to our outpatient department with a complaint of chest pain that started suddenly 2 hours ago. The pain is described as a sharp, stabbing sensation that radiates to the left arm and is 8/10 in severity. The patient is alert and oriented to time, place, and person.

Dr. Debasis Roy General Physician, Apollo Hospital Chennai Apollo Hospital Chennai Napier Town, Chennai - 600 004 Phone: 044-2829 2020 / 2829 2000 Fax: 044-2829 2001 Email: [apchennai@apollohospital.com](mailto:apchennai@apollohospital.com) website: www.apollohospitals.com Prescription Note Name: Arjun Mehta w/o Pradeep Pattnaik Age: 30-year-old Admitted under: Emergency Department Date: 29th May 2021 Consultant Physician: Dr. S. Krishnan Phone: 9581618110 ABHA ID: 26-7973-1394-2969 Chief Complaint: Patient reports episodes of dizziness. Vital Parameters: BP: 120/80 mmHg, Pulse: 72 bpm, Temperature: 36.8°C, Respiratory Rate: 16 breaths/min. Investigations: - Blood Tests: To be ordered - ECG: To be ordered - MRI/CT Scan: To be ordered Assessment: The patient is suffering from episodes of dizziness. The cause is currently under investigation. Plan: 1. Blood Tests to be ordered to rule out anemia and other hematological abnormalities. 2. ECG to be ordered to rule out cardiac arrhythmias. 3. MRI/CT Scan to be ordered to rule out intracranial pathology. 4. Patient is to be kept under observation with continuous monitoring of vital parameters. 5. Medication: - Metoclopramide 10 mg IV stat for symptomatic relief of dizziness - Propranolol 10 mg orally once daily for prophylaxis against cardiac arrhythmias - Ferrous sulfate 100 mg orally once daily as an iron supplement Follow-up: - Patient to be reviewed in 24 hours for further assessment and management. Signature of Doctor: ______________________ Name of Doctor: Dr. S. Krishnan Qualifications: MD (General Medicine) Designation: Consultant Physician Date: 29th May 2021 Note: This is a sample prescription note and should not be used for actual clinical practice.

The patient, Lakshmi Narayanan w/o Ramesh Kumar, was admitted under the care of Dr. Arindam Ghosh on ______ for evaluation and management of fever and chills. The patient presented with a history of high-grade fever and chills for the past 5 days. Initial investigations revealed elevated white blood cell count with no evidence of any significant infection. The patient received treatment as per the hospital protocol, which included administration of IV fluids, broad-spectrum antibiotics, and supportive care. The patient's condition showed significant improvement with resolution of fever and chills. The patient was closely monitored for any signs of complications and was discharged once stable.
- Patient Name: Priya Gupta w/o Subramaniam Venkataraman - Age: 33-year-old - ABHA ID: 57-2203-3700-9693 - Contact Details: Phone: 9548396312

Priya Gupta w/o Subramaniam Venkataraman, a 33-year-old female, was admitted to Apollo Hospital Chennai on 12 April 2023 with a chief complaint of persistent cough. She was evaluated by Dr. S. Krishnan, Consultant Pulmonologist, and her treatment plan was as follows: - Initial diagnosis: Chronic Bronchitis - Treatment: Antibiotics (Ceftriaxone 1g IV BD for 5 days) and Bronchodilators (Salbutamol 2.5mg IV as required) - Investigations: Complete Blood Count (CBC), Chest X-ray (PA view), and Sputum examination
